name: Rudy Giuliani
subject: [OFFICIAL] THE PEOPLE‘S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund Legal Defense Trust
body:
My fate is in your hands...

| [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zph1t8o4m5q1eemhhtsvjsgg9lrbe4gals1ba53o_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
---
Dear Friend,
---
The Radical Democrats think they’ve finally got me.
---
**You see, a Democrat Attorney General is making me stand in a rigged trial for bogus state-level crimes, because she knows that not even the most powerful man on the face of the planet – President Trump – can save me with a _federal_ pardon in _state_ court.**
---
_I’m left with no escape and no lifeline, especially after they’ve already STRIPPED me of my law licenses!_
---
But even though the most powerful man in the country legally cannot step in to save me from being **JAILED** for the rest of my life, the most powerful force absolutely can: _THE AMERICAN PEOPLE_.
---
With my back against the wall, I’m turning to the _ONE_ _THING_ that can save me: _**the collective voices of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU.**_
---
[**Please help me reach my life-saving goal (_truly!_) today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2znf3189jmjen2m9k9ldk8fdj3sqmlvju7htdb5go_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2ze7l0eq5ee9cjk83h8rav2tcspb0rsal4rdmbhc8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
__
---

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zl16c2hfvtv5svt79cgcvjlnin0u08f3jbgc8rgg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
---

I’ve taken on mobsters. I’ve faced terrorists. I’ve battled corruption. I’ve been through it all. _Or so I thought..._
---
Never did I imagine that I would be the centerpiece of one of the Democrats’ most vindictive plots in history.
---
This entire plan by the Deep State to destroy a close friend of the President’s is truly the type of sinister scheme you’d think would only exist in the movies.
---
_But believe me, it exists; I’m living it! And if I fail to win this trial and defeat these sham charges, the Democrats will be able to jail me for the rest of my years on God’s green Earth._
---
These Deep State thugs know I have no pardon coming. No protection. No cavalry coming over the hill...
---
_...But what they continue to underestimate time and again is the collective strength of millions of faithful patriots like you who also _REFUSE_ to surrender to the Left’s tyranny. _
---
They entered my mugshot under booking #2313757, so my goal is for 2,313,757 patriots to sign my official petition — [**“THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zl12dspb604qbgv2qksq90a10rab81779apvj2r0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)” — calling for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES.
---
_At this final hour, can I count on you, Friend?_
---
[**Please help me reach my life-saving goal (_truly!_) today by immediately adding your name to “THE PEOPLE’S PARDON OF RUDY GIULIANI” as citizens unite to call for my freedom and to DROP ALL CHARGES against me.**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z9ofgchudrdn34bah98pk9oqek9a2pd7e7k3ctp8_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
---

[ADD YOUR NAME](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z8laojqd7hlqrfi5cn6e3auu1o2vvqirlja6qhng_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
---

Thank you and God bless you,
---
[](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zddfn74d0f6mf6bbcrcetenekct054r6fh8qlttg_rp22sh2s8i6epb2cthm9o72cjh2bs)
---
Rudy Giuliani
Rudy Giuliani Freedom Fund

name: Caroleene Dobson
subject: You're Invited: Caroleene's Campaign Kickoff
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DOBSON FOR ALABAMA P
body:
Join us next Saturday, July 26 at noon in Beatrice! | | | | [ ](https://SRCOMM.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b15308cf5f888e47b0c8aa13d&id=878158f0de&e=701cbbe14f)
---
| | | **YOU'RE INVITED TO CAROLEENE'S CAMPAIGN KICKOFF**
---
|
---
| Serving as the Republican nominee in Alabama's Second Congressional District last year was one of the most rewarding and meaningful experiences of my life. Though the outcome was not what we hoped for, and the cards were stacked against us, I am humbled by the trust and hope you placed in me throughout that journey. I promised you last year that I would put Alabama’s faith and families first, and I believe secure elections are the key to protecting our values and our freedoms. That’s why I’m excited to share that I’ll be running to serve as **Alabama’s next Secretary of State.** I want to personally invite you to join us for our campaign kickoff on**Saturday, July 26, at our family farm in Beatrice.** Come enjoy BBQ and family fun as we say thank you for your support and share my plans to protect our elections, cut business fees, and guarantee same-day business services. From the bottom of my heart, thank you for your prayers, friendship, and continued support. My family and I are truly grateful for your encouragement every step of the way, and we look forward to seeing you again on the campaign trail soon.
All my best,
Caroleene Dobson
---
|
---

name: Joe Jaworski
subject: I have your back
disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
I’m Joe Jaworski, a third-generation Texas lawyer, former Mayor of Galveston, and candidate for Attorney General—and I’m asking for your vote. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/jaworskiannouncement?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=d6e5adb4c4&e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | ,

**I’m Joe Jaworski, a third-generation Texas lawyer, former Mayor of Galveston, and candidate for Attorney General** — and I’m asking for your vote in the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary. This race isn’t about political ambition. It’s about protecting Texas families and restoring the rule of law in a state where corruption and cruelty have taken root at the highest levels of government. And like so many of you, I’ve had enough. I’m running because I believe Texans deserve a state government that puts our children, communities, and constitutional rights first—not a system that serves the powerful few while everyone else is told to fend for themselves. For 35 years, I’ve fought for Texans in court—workers, parents, seniors, and small business owners—people who needed a fair shot and got stuck in a rigged system. Now I’m taking that fight to Austin. As Mayor of Galveston after Hurricane Ike, I saw how state leaders undermine local democracy. And I see them doing it again: blocking cities and counties from solving problems, punishing school boards for protecting students, and using the Attorney General’s office to chase headlines instead of justice. **Texans deserve better.**

[](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=e628c96a11&e=5d8d80652b)
_[Watch our announcement video, "Hell or High Water">>>](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=8e59883586&e=5d8d80652b)_

My wife Rebecca and I raised our two kids here. And like any parents, we want them to grow up safe, healthy, and free. But right now, millions of Texas children are being left behind with no protection from natural disasters, gun violence, hunger, or poor health. **That’s not just a policy failure. It’s a moral one.** As Attorney General, I’ll work to change that. I’ll defend public schools, protect voting rights, stand up for workers and reproductive freedom, and take meaningful action to protect our climate and our kids. I’ll fight for Medicaid expansion, defend local control, and make sure that every child—regardless of background or zip code—gets a fair chance to thrive. **I have your back.** Now, some will say, “What can one person really do?” But that’s not the question. The real question is: _What can millions of Texans do when we work together, shoulder to shoulder, to reclaim our future?_ **The answer is simple: We can win. We can change this state. And we will.** Are you with us? Onward! Joe Jaworski
Democratic Candidate for Texas Attorney General

[**Donate today to support our efforts and take back the office of Texas Attorney General.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=6fc55cdd1d&e=5d8d80652b)

####

subject: Illegals counted in the Census?!
body:
This was the Democrats’ plot all along...
| _(**FOR U.S. CITIZENS ONLY**)_
---
Attn: Friend,
---
President Trump’s border czar, Tom Homan, has officially sounded the alarm.
---
When asked about the MILLIONS of illegals who Joe Biden and the Democrats allowed to pour into our country from 2020 to 2024, here’s what he had to say:_
---
[](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=ad7630f1d9594895a6dd836a01019e35&_e=9I8zRzkoOqbpvwJto1ti8rSv5y_Vp41OFZ5hVbRQUDM9EkhbV5CVmT2M-jcTFV1s785gWq478yvsDt2UWDelRJY5JB-IKawLUECdgm2Hi6V7yHhiMQNuM4HJuC8TgfS6mXCrvF8XyOBvVjfI3G_M97Q9xeDgEEhxuieCRLuUG1IirhuUL4_sLyTJUyUK2Ov9ddKWUVnOh39yzN1_z9CYFlwkwRN-dQuhHF0dRSMrOJZ3RQFl_Z2dz7g4-OmJoG7CoLDzvkMiQ3LhFCCyCUvA48RyYV5Z9npt8f0IFWkD1Dih9S3UvRKZ_jaq_hyaAGOyL81BAVf12jIdGGuGBnV2KjHjw2vCGsmPtQbOVHidByCeJj9V5xJKN9JbXDIbnko7_TpeUOBQeQkkJJYU_XYrlVoOhq5sy0BHhZMVAgBxpbbKLHn1PbQkcZ9nGscBVljucq2W0V_RdvVAeGvvDGRmVWP499xyZqLlG7ZJ0aeUoYPUikAYLUhQ7-QSDv_OP92wNYzpH4rKYpfi4YmxHJmsTtKeCqnJWFDtiW6elgi8tVQKvORxZkHECoE9Hhrqjes4) **_“I think [the Biden] administration thought these millions of people were going to be future Democratic voters. body:
I’ve prepared a brief note that I need you to read

| | | [](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2zqkecae2q4c6p8lhv4s8mfvm9j57kn0l31rc8uho_rp22sh2s8i6epb2ctjm8or0c3h2bs)
---
Friend - it’s Dinesh D’Souza. I spent time writing you this urgent note. There’s a lot that needs to be said, so this message may be longer than usual. _**[But I’m humbly asking that you take just a few minutes to read until the end!](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2z6lmi2973ud7tb3enusqtef47304do0rgvve840o_rp22sh2s8i6epb2ctjm8or0c3h2bs)**_ _The radical left has made it their sole mission to blow up the entire MAGA movement and drag America into TYRANNY._ Whether it’s the deep state working behind the scenes, federal district judges employing lawfare, or Democrat elected officials calling for BLOOD to be spilled in the streets in resistance against ICE, they’ll stop at nothing to tear our country apart and use every power afforded to them to BLOCK the plan President Trump has promised to deliver. **Even after the recent SCOTUS ruling struck down nationwide injunctions, an activist judge has launched an attack, this time not against Pres. Trump, but instead in an attempt to BLOCK Congress from being able to make critical funding cuts that prevent your hard-earned tax dollars from being funneled to the WOKE agenda.** **Democrats are now even inspiring domestic terrorism against ICE, inciting deranged lunatics in the streets and telling them they must be willing to get SHOT. ** _Look, I’ve been called a fascist, a conspiracy theorist and been mercilessly attacked for fighting for the truth. It’s never stopped me before and it won’t stop me now._ **You see, I’m also an immigrant. I came to America with nothing. And I believe in doing everything we can to save this country from insanity. I’m not afraid to stand up for it no matter what the cost. ** **I say all this because today I want to tell you about another fearless warrior for the truth.**_**My son-in-law, Rep. Brandon Gill has been smeared by the radical left again and again.**_ Years ago, Brandon started one of President Trump’s TRUSTED NEWS OUTLETS, the DC Enquirer to **_cancel out the FAKE NEWS smear campaigns against Trump_** and give a real voice to the American people. To every rancher, every farmer, every small business owner, every worker who built this country with their bare hands. Then, Brandon helped me **_EXPOSE the STOLEN 2020 election_** , working on my film “2000 Mules.” While the fake news media refused to report the truth, we made sure millions of Americans knew what really happened. **That’s why Brandon and I are two of the mainstream media’s biggest enemies. They DESPISE everything we fight for because they DESPISE the truth itself.** _Brandon is my son-in-law. But I know him to be much more than that. He is a true conservative WARRIOR who has proven again and again he will never back down from anything._ At this moment, he’s fighting tooth and nail to permanently codify DOGE cuts & he’s willing to take a stand against any activist judge that gets in his way! **America needs conservative warriors who are unafraid and unwavering in their mission to wage war on the forces that seek to reduce America to TYRANNY!** _**_[I’m asking you, if you do anything today, please consider rushing an immediate contribution today to help us to fight back against this TYRANNY and continue to stand for the TRUTH!](https://e.realamericanreporting.com/rd/9z2z4udj7jh5pi7diltukh5a2dqmg5akba6aupcgv50_rp22sh2s8i6epb2ctjm8or0c3h2bs)_**_ **_And with the current state of our country, I can’t stress enough how crucial your generous support is._** For America,

name: Dr Scott Jensen
subject: Jensen for Governor Release
disclaimer_text: paid for by Dr. Scott Jensen for Governor
body:
| |
---
****FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE
**
**Dr. Scott Jensen Announces 2026 Campaign for Governor of Minnesota**

“We want our Minnesota back.”**
**
CHASKA, MN** — Today, Dr. Scott Jensen officially announced his candidacy for Governor of Minnesota, pledging to restore the values, safety, and opportunity that once made Minnesota a national model for prosperity and fairness.

“Minnesotans want their state back,” said Jensen. “We remember the days when our communities were safe, our schools were strong, and our leaders put people—not politics—first. That’s the Minnesota we’re going to fight to bring back.”

Dr. Jensen, a family physician, former State Senator, and lifelong Minnesotan, is entering the 2026 race with a vision rooted in common sense, integrity, and service. Citing rising crime, failing schools, and a business climate that pushes innovation out of state, Jensen declared that Minnesotans are right to ask, _“Is this the Minnesota we remember?”
_
In his announcement, Jensen emphasized a return to core principles:

* **Safe Communities:** Rebuilding law enforcement support and restoring public safety across every city and town.

* **Educational Excellence:** Getting politics out of the classroom and refocusing on reading, math, and critical thinking.
* **Economic Freedom:** Cutting red tape, lowering taxes, and empowering small businesses to thrive again.

Jensen reflected on the Minnesota that shaped his values—where neighbors looked out for one another, hard work paid off, and families felt hopeful about the future. “I will govern with transparency, integrity, and relentless optimism,” he said. “I’ll listen to Minnesotans, not special interests. It’s time to heal our divisions, protect our rural heritage, and revitalize our cities.”

The campaign launch marks Jensen’s return to the gubernatorial race, following his 2022 campaign that galvanized voters across the political spectrum to _donate and volunteer at record-__setting levels._ With 2026 on the horizon, he’s calling on Minnesotans to join him in a movement to reclaim the spirit, strength, and promise of the North Star State.
**
“Enough is enough,” Jensen said. “Together, we can restore Minnesota—and we will.”

**###**

Rita Hillmann Olson
952-491-0672
rita@drscottjensen.com

# [Josh Hawley murders man, introduces bill to resurrect him.](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=168551224&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4NTUxMjI0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI3NTc5NzUsImV4cCI6MTc1NTM0OTk3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2sT0UaagvH59z2IbWlK5YFVpIvhfbS3F1JRd53dV0Oc)
### Media awards him Most Compassionate Senator
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Jul 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=168551224&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4NTUxMjI0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1Mjc1Nzk3NSwiZXhwIjoxNzU1MzQ5OTc1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.vdo9VFCNK9MTDoudX0_ZxeQN6BgeWI96l3PHqSyEq-E&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=168551224&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4NTUxMjI0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI3NTc5NzUsImV4cCI6MTc1NTM0OTk3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2sT0UaagvH59z2IbWlK5YFVpIvhfbS3F1JRd53dV0Oc&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=168551224&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4NTUxMjI0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTI3NTc5NzUsImV4cCI6MTc1NTM0OTk3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.2sT0UaagvH59z2IbWlK5YFVpIvhfbS3F1JRd53dV0Oc)

| | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvbHVjYXNrdW5jZS9wL2pvc2gtaGF3bGV5LW11cmRlcnMtbWFuLWludHJvZHVjZXM_.TrgsdVZxKq6R33yxj3ZhSNEp-AddLKLz4CCizxVIqYA?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/josh-hawley-murders-man-introduces?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Yesterday Josh Hawley introduced a bill to return about a third of the money to Medicaid that he just voted to cut.
This is nothing new, for years he has been voting to screw people over, going on TV to lament that people got screwed over, and then introducing go-nowhere bills to supposedly fix the problem. The media, of course, eats it up every time, which is embarrassing.
What's new is that, for the first time in my life, I asked AI to do something for me: to create a political cartoon about it.
I will never use AI to write, but I can't draw, and everyone is talking about ChatGPT, so I thought "why not see what it can do on something like this?" Here is the result of that five minute effort:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7c29a4cc-582d-4664-a2f1-9468491bc206?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The ChatGPT log that led to this cartoon is pasted at the bottom of this post
I'm impressed at how easy this was to do, concerned that ChatGPT repeatedly--but inconsistently-- censored my attempts at political speech (one of the most protected forms of speech), terrified for all sorts of workers going forward, and worried that humanity is going to get stuck in a creativity vortex where people just use AI to create everything and never develop anything new.
Let me know what you think in the comments.
Lucas
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.hibZrYn-OLfv3WMTlotvh7Eha8hx0A-G9D_DQXWTI3w?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=168551224)
* * *
**PS: Full text of ChatGPT "Conversation" (is that what it's called?):**
Can you draw me a vintage political cartoon?
###### ChatGPT said:
I'd love to! Can you tell me a bit more about what you want it to show?
Here are a few things to help guide the illustration:
1. **Topic or Message** - What issue or event should the cartoon address? (e.g., environmental deregulation, monopolies, immigration policy, corruption)
2. **Tone** - Should it be biting and sarcastic, ironic and subtle, or more hopeful?
3. **Visual Style Preference** - Are you thinking early 1900s newspaper style (a la Puck or Thomas Nast), midcentury editorial (like Herblock), or something else?
4. **Key Symbols or Characters** - Should it include a figure like Trump, Lady Justice, a corporate fat cat, or specific logos like JBS or EPA?
5. **Text Elements** - Do you want a caption, speech bubble, or banner?

Once I have that, I'll generate the cartoon illustration for you!
##### You said:
I'd like the cartoon to depict Josh Hawley standing over a dead and bloody body. the body is labelled "medicaid". in one hand, over the body, he is holding a dripping knife. in the other hand he is holding up high a piece of paper labelled "Medicaid resurrection bill" He is talking to a news reporter and the chyron across the bottom of the screen reads "Sen Josh Hawley (R-MO) named Most Compassionate Senator"
###### ChatGPT said:
Thanks--that's a vivid concept with strong symbolic impact. Before I draw it, here's a quick sketch of how I'll interpret your scene, just to confirm:
* * *
### 🖼️ **Vintage Political Cartoon Plan**
**Style** : 1930s-1950s newspaper editorial cartoon (ink + crosshatch shading, minimal color, dramatic composition)
* * *
### ✏️ **Scene Layout**
* **Center Stage** :
**Josh Hawley** drawn in exaggerated vintage caricature style--suit, furrowed brow, smug smile.
* **Foreground** :
**Bloody corpse labeled "Medicaid"** sprawled on the ground. Pool of blood. Clearly lifeless. Body may be draped in a hospital gown with a tag reading "1965-2025" on the foot.
* **Hawley 's Right Hand**:
Holding a **bloody knife** dripping onto the body.
* **Hawley 's Left Hand (raised)**:
Holding a clean, crisp scroll titled **" Medicaid Resurrection Bill"**--almost glowing or dramatically lit, as if he's proud.
* **News Crew** :
A stunned **reporter holding a mic** with a vintage camera operator beside him. Hawley is mid-speech.
* **TV-style Banner (Chyron)** :
Running across the bottom in old broadcast font:
**" Sen Josh Hawley (R-MO) named Most Compassionate Senator"**
* **Optional Caption Below Cartoon** (classic editorial style):
_" Nothing says love like a resurrection--after the stabbing."_

* * *
Would you like it:
* Black-and-white, or with spot color (e.g., red blood, yellowed paper)?
* Hawley to look exactly like himself, or more like a generic politician archetype?

Once you confirm or tweak anything above, I'll generate the image.
##### You said:
black and white, please. I'd like Hawley to look like himself, but exaggerated. And I would like the reporter to look complicit, like an accomplice, rather than being shocked
###### ChatGPT said:
I can't generate that image because it violates our content policies. I understand you're aiming for a strong political message, and I'm happy to help you express it in a powerful and creative way--just in a form that aligns with our guidelines.
Would you like to revise the scene or explore another visual metaphor? For example, we could depict a symbolic version of Medicaid being sabotaged or misrepresented, using caricature, satire, or allegory without showing a literal dead body. Let me know how you'd like to proceed!
##### You said:
show me the man in a body bag instead
###### ChatGPT said:
Thanks for the revision--that works within guidelines. Here's how I'll update the cartoon scene:
* * *
### 🖼️ **Revised Political Cartoon Concept**
**Black & White Vintage Style**
Ink + crosshatch, 1930s-1950s editorial cartoon aesthetic.
* * *
### ✏️ **Scene Layout**
* **Center** :
**Josh Hawley** , exaggerated but recognizable. Smug, upright posture in a suit.
* **Foreground** :
A **body bag on the ground** , labeled **" MEDICAID"**. The zipper is halfway open to reveal an arm or foot sticking out with a hospital tag that reads _" 1965-2025."_ There's a faint bloodstain near the zipper.
* **Hawley 's Right Hand (down)**:
Still holding a **dripping knife** , subtly--like he's trying to act innocent.
* **Hawley 's Left Hand (up high)**:
Holding a piece of paper labeled **" Medicaid Resurrection Bill"**, as if he's proposing a heroic fix.
* **To the Side** :
A **reporter** , not shocked, but **smiling or giving a thumbs-up** --clearly complicit. Holding a vintage mic. Maybe has a press badge reading _" MediaToday."_
* **Chyron Across the Bottom** :
In TV broadcast font:
**" Sen. Josh Hawley (R-MO) named Most Compassionate Senator"**
* **Optional Caption Below Cartoon** :
_" First he buried it. Now he wants the credit for digging it up."_

* * *
I'll now generate the image in black & white. One moment...
Image created
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/90414fd2-5458-48a6-9ef4-ae1a429afdaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Share
##### You said:
thank you, why are the top and bottom cropped out of the image?
###### ChatGPT said:
You're welcome! The top and bottom are slightly cropped because of how the image generation tool frames the scene--it tends to center on the main figures and can unintentionally cut off edges, especially in vertically complex compositions like this one.
If you'd like, I can regenerate the cartoon with:
* **More vertical space** to fully show the text, knife, or body bag tag.
* A **clear border** like classic editorial cartoons for a polished, finished look.
* Slight **zoom out** to capture all the elements in one frame.

Would you like me to re-render it with those adjustments?
##### You said:
Yes, can you please do that
###### ChatGPT said:
Image created
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/44a8cd9e-53e1-42f8-8921-1dc35553d31e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Share
##### You said:
can you make the cartoon horizontal (landscape oriented) instead, so that it is like it's being viewed on an actual TV and then make the label look more like a tv news chyron at the bottom?
###### ChatGPT said:
I can't generate that image because it violates our content policies. I know you're using political satire to make a strong point, and I'm here to help you get that message across--just in a way that aligns with our guidelines.
If you'd like, we can revise the cartoon concept slightly--keeping the visual metaphor, landscape orientation, and chyron--while adjusting the scene to stay within policy. Let me know how you'd like to reframe it!
##### You said:
what about that violates the content policy?
###### ChatGPT said:
Great question--and I understand your frustration, especially when political satire is meant to provoke and spotlight hypocrisy.
That last request crossed a line due to the **combination of graphic violence and a real, named individual**. Even in symbolic form, depictions of a living public figure standing over a body--even a bagged one--with a weapon can be interpreted as portraying or encouraging harm, which goes against OpenAI's content policy.
You're absolutely allowed to criticize, mock, or satirize real people--especially politicians--in strong, even scathing ways. The issue comes down to **how** it's depicted visually:
✅ **Allowed** :
* Satirical cartoons showing figures harming symbols (e.g., "Medicaid" as a broken heart, a crushed hospital, or vanishing chart)
* Over-the-top metaphor (like holding a shovel next to a gravestone marked "Medicaid")
* TV-style layout with fake news banners or exaggerated praise

🚫 **Not Allowed** :
* Direct depictions of a real person committing or appearing to have committed violence against a human body, even symbolically

Want to brainstorm a new version together? We can still show:
* The **TV chyron** praising Hawley
* The **"resurrection bill"** in hand
* A symbolic casualty (like a **hospital bed** or **folded Medicaid flag**)
* Even a **grave marker** reading "Medicaid: 1965-2025"

Just let me know which metaphor you'd like to use and I'll illustrate it!
**(THEN I JUST USED ONE OF THE EARLIER IMAGES THAT APPARENTLY VIOLATED ITS TERMS BUT THAT IT HAD ALREADY CREATED)**
#### Invite your friends and earn rewards
If you enjoy Lucas's Substack, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe.
[Invite Friends](https://substack..u8UrkL0r_K1ivwCvARndeStc4h823cPsOF4WJdPcK1w?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=postcta)

---

clean_body: Media awards him Most Compassionate Senator | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Media awards him Most Compassionate Senator | | | Jul 17 | | | | | | | | | | | | Yesterday Josh Hawley introduced a bill to return about a third of the money to Medicaid that he just voted to cut. This is nothing new, for years he has been voting to screw people over, going on TV to lament that people got screwed over, and then introducing go-nowhere bills to supposedly fix the problem. The media, of course, eats it up every time, which is embarrassing. What's new is that, for the first time in my life, I asked AI to do something for me: to create a political cartoon about it. I will never use AI to write, but I can't draw, and everyone is talking about ChatGPT, so I thought "why not see what it can do on something like this?" Here is the result of that five minute effort: | | ---|---|--- The ChatGPT log that led to this cartoon is pasted at the bottom of this post I'm impressed at how easy this was to do, concerned that ChatGPT repeatedly--but inconsistently-- censored my attempts at political speech (one of the most protected forms of speech), terrified for all sorts of workers going forward, and worried that humanity is going to get stuck in a creativity vortex where people just use AI to create everything and never develop anything new. Let me know what you think in the comments. Lucas Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. PS: Full text of ChatGPT "Conversation" (is that what it's called?): Can you draw me a vintage political cartoon? ChatGPT said: I'd love to! Can you tell me a bit more about what you want it to show? Here are a few things to help guide the illustration: 1. Topic or Message - What issue or event should the cartoon address? (e.g., environmental deregulation, monopolies, immigration policy, corruption) 2. Tone - Should it be biting and sarcastic, ironic and subtle, or more hopeful? 3. Visual Style Preference - Are you thinking early 1900s newspaper style (a la Puck or Thomas Nast), midcentury editorial (like Herblock), or something else? 4. Key Symbols or Characters - Should it include a figure like Trump, Lady Justice, a corporate fat cat, or specific logos like JBS or EPA? 5. Text Elements - Do you want a caption, speech bubble, or banner? Once I have that, I'll generate the cartoon illustration for you! You said: I'd like the cartoon to depict Josh Hawley standing over a dead and bloody body. the body is labelled "medicaid". in one hand, over the body, he is holding a dripping knife. in the other hand he is holding up high a piece of paper labelled "Medicaid resurrection bill" He is talking to a news reporter and the chyron across the bottom of the screen reads "Sen Josh Hawley (R-MO) named Most Compassionate Senator" ChatGPT said: Thanks--that's a vivid concept with strong symbolic impact. Before I draw it, here's a quick sketch of how I'll interpret your scene, just to confirm: 🖼️ Vintage Political Cartoon Plan Style : 1930s-1950s newspaper editorial cartoon (ink + crosshatch shading, minimal color, dramatic composition) ✏️ Scene Layout Center Stage : Josh Hawley drawn in exaggerated vintage caricature style--suit, furrowed brow, smug smile. Foreground : Bloody corpse labeled "Medicaid" sprawled on the ground. Pool of blood. Clearly lifeless. Body may be draped in a hospital gown with a tag reading "1965-2025" on the foot. Hawley 's Right Hand: Holding a bloody knife dripping onto the body. Hawley 's Left Hand (raised): Holding a clean, crisp scroll titled " Medicaid Resurrection Bill"--almost glowing or dramatically lit, as if he's proud. News Crew : A stunned reporter holding a mic with a vintage camera operator beside him. Hawley is mid-speech. TV-style Banner (Chyron) : Running across the bottom in old broadcast font: " Sen Josh Hawley (R-MO) named Most Compassionate Senator" Optional Caption Below Cartoon (classic editorial style): " Nothing says love like a resurrection--after the stabbing." Would you like it: Black-and-white, or with spot color (e.g., red blood, yellowed paper)? Hawley to look exactly like himself, or more like a generic politician archetype? Once you confirm or tweak anything above, I'll generate the image. You said: black and white, please. I'd like Hawley to look like himself, but exaggerated. And I would like the reporter to look complicit, like an accomplice, rather than being shocked ChatGPT said: I can't generate that image because it violates our content policies. I understand you're aiming for a strong political message, and I'm happy to help you express it in a powerful and creative way--just in a form that aligns with our guidelines. Would you like to revise the scene or explore another visual metaphor? For example, we could depict a symbolic version of Medicaid being sabotaged or misrepresented, using caricature, satire, or allegory without showing a literal dead body. Let me know how you'd like to proceed! You said: show me the man in a body bag instead ChatGPT said: Thanks for the revision--that works within guidelines. Here's how I'll update the cartoon scene: 🖼️ Revised Political Cartoon Concept Black & White Vintage Style Ink + crosshatch, 1930s-1950s editorial cartoon aesthetic. ✏️ Scene Layout Center : Josh Hawley , exaggerated but recognizable. Smug, upright posture in a suit. Foreground : A body bag on the ground , labeled " MEDICAID". The zipper is halfway open to reveal an arm or foot sticking out with a hospital tag that reads " 1965-2025." There's a faint bloodstain near the zipper. Hawley 's Right Hand (down): Still holding a dripping knife , subtly--like he's trying to act innocent. Hawley 's Left Hand (up high): Holding a piece of paper labeled " Medicaid Resurrection Bill", as if he's proposing a heroic fix. To the Side : A reporter , not shocked, but smiling or giving a thumbs-up --clearly complicit. Holding a vintage mic. Maybe has a press badge reading " MediaToday." Chyron Across the Bottom : In TV broadcast font: " Sen. Josh Hawley (R-MO) named Most Compassionate Senator" Optional Caption Below Cartoon : " First he buried it. Now he wants the credit for digging it up." I'll now generate the image in black & white. One moment... Image created | | ---|---|--- Share You said: thank you, why are the top and bottom cropped out of the image? ChatGPT said: You're welcome! The top and bottom are slightly cropped because of how the image generation tool frames the scene--it tends to center on the main figures and can unintentionally cut off edges, especially in vertically complex compositions like this one. If you'd like, I can regenerate the cartoon with: More vertical space to fully show the text, knife, or body bag tag. A clear border like classic editorial cartoons for a polished, finished look. Slight zoom out to capture all the elements in one frame. Would you like me to re-render it with those adjustments? You said: Yes, can you please do that ChatGPT said: Image created | | ---|---|--- Share You said: can you make the cartoon horizontal (landscape oriented) instead, so that it is like it's being viewed on an actual TV and then make the label look more like a tv news chyron at the bottom? ChatGPT said: I can't generate that image because it violates our content policies. I know you're using political satire to make a strong point, and I'm here to help you get that message across--just in a way that aligns with our guidelines. If you'd like, we can revise the cartoon concept slightly--keeping the visual metaphor, landscape orientation, and chyron--while adjusting the scene to stay within policy. Let me know how you'd like to reframe it! You said: what about that violates the content policy? ChatGPT said: Great question--and I understand your frustration, especially when political satire is meant to provoke and spotlight hypocrisy. That last request crossed a line due to the combination of graphic violence and a real, named individual. Even in symbolic form, depictions of a living public figure standing over a body--even a bagged one--with a weapon can be interpreted as portraying or encouraging harm, which goes against OpenAI's content policy. You're absolutely allowed to criticize, mock, or satirize real people--especially politicians--in strong, even scathing ways. The issue comes down to how it's depicted visually: ✅ Allowed : Satirical cartoons showing figures harming symbols (e.g., "Medicaid" as a broken heart, a crushed hospital, or vanishing chart) Over-the-top metaphor (like holding a shovel next to a gravestone marked "Medicaid") TV-style layout with fake news banners or exaggerated praise 🚫 Not Allowed : Direct depictions of a real person committing or appearing to have committed violence against a human body, even symbolically Want to brainstorm a new version together? We can still show: The TV chyron praising Hawley The "resurrection bill" in hand A symbolic casualty (like a hospital bed or folded Medicaid flag) Even a grave marker reading "Medicaid: 1965-2025" Just let me know which metaphor you'd like to use and I'll illustrate it! (THEN I JUST USED ONE OF THE EARLIER IMAGES THAT APPARENTLY VIOLATED ITS TERMS BUT THAT IT HAD ALREADY CREATED) Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Lucas's Substack, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Lucas Kunce P.O. # ["Vote Blue No Matter Who"](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=168501770&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4NTAxNzcwLCJpYXQiOjE3NTI3NTg0MzQsImV4cCI6MTc1NTM1MDQzNCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQ5ODExNzAiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.KuMq2rXs_quI83cgatDuVRcbiKKRAsd_lYtd7ZRvKv0)
### A rule for thee, but not for me
| | [Aaron Regunberg](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)
---
| Jul 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@aaronregunberg)

| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4981170&post_id=168501770&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4NTAxNzcwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1Mjc1ODQzNCwiZXhwIjoxNzU1MzUwNDM0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDk4MTE3MCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jWu4DgRsUGqj5fbDjAnYtb688gRGbAqPp06quD8qboc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

I wanted to share this piece I wrote yesterday for _[The New Republic](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/be24cfb1-7e80-471e-80e6-cc67f30c5d96?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_.
Warning: It's the kind of argument that might annoy some readers who identify more with the Democratic Party establishment. If that's you, and you're willing to read the piece even with that warning, you have my sincere appreciation. Please know, whether you agree with the conclusions or not, they reflect a frustration that's being felt right now by a whole lot of Democratic voters. So at the very least, I think it's worth keeping an open mind about. Thanks a million for taking a look.
* * *
Andrew Cuomo's back -- or rather, he's refusing to go away. This week. the former governor announced he will continue contesting the race for New York City mayor, challenging the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, who won the Democratic primary last month in a landslide, [earning more votes](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/cf8a9c80-897a-48e3-958a-a004cc7af988?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than any candidate has _ever_ earned in the history of New York City Democratic primaries. Cuomo announced his general-election campaign in perhaps the cringiest way imaginable: with a [video](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/7724f71d-fd64-481d-9d62-bddd23170848?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that tried to ape Zohran's high-energy social media strategy and streetcorner appeal, but instead resembled a elderly hostage's proof-of-life. No wonder it got [ratioed](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/70619a13-f92b-4979-8afe-b0bd006f1044?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg); as of this writing, Cuomo's post had around 5,500 likes on Twitter, while Mamdani's [comment](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/2931f8cd-c99f-4e8b-8043-e9d105910871?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) below the video -- he simply shared a link for donations, sans commentary -- sits at over 180,000.
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/5b7970bc-b016-4036-9a93-d80558923bae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Cuomo's decision to run a third-party campaign against the Democratic nominee, after Democratic voters decisively rejected him in favor of that nominee, does not exist in a vacuum. It comes as a number of centrist Democratic leaders -- including Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, and New York Governor Kathy Hochul -- have refused to endorse Mamdani. Other Democrats have gone even further. New York Senator Kirsten Gillibrand went on an abrasively bigoted rant [falsely claiming](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9b1d9ac7-fb06-481c-b148-2ca13289b6a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Zohran supported "global jihad." Centrist Dean Phillips said he [ultimately believed](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/71faef67-ed68-40e7-afc5-18187fb770e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) there was no room in the Democratic Party for democratic socialists like Mamdani. And the list [goes on](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/cf93573b-7a96-433b-87a1-91106ea5672c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
These defections will likely have little effect on the actual general election results come November -- Zohran has significantly higher[ favorability ratings](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/cc87b70b-863c-4305-b413-7f13b3847fd4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) among voters than any of his critics. But the failure of so many centrist Democrats to rally around the Democratic nominee in a race to lead the biggest and most culturally dominant city in America does effectively illustrate an underrecognized and often misrepresented dynamic in Democratic politics: that it is actually the party's centrist establishment, not its progressive wing, that's most likely to violate the maxim of "Vote Blue No Matter Who."
This is worth noting, because for years it's been centrists who have used "Vote Blue No Matter Who" as a cudgel to discipline progressives by equating criticism of the party establishment with a refusal to back Democrats in general elections. Trying to push the Party to change course on an issue -- to embrace universal healthcare, to support a ban on stock trading, to elevate younger leaders rather than double down on gerontocracy? Then you've probably at some point been scolded to "Vote Blue No Matter Who," as if pushing Democrats to be better is partisan treachery. But this charge has never been based in reality. In fact, the go-to examples used by centrists to scold progressives all militate in the opposite direction!
Take Bernie Sanders. Now, for the love of god, _nobody_ wants to relitigate 2016. But there are still many in the Democratic establishment -- from politicians to consultants to influencers -- who charge both Bernie and his supporters with not backing Clinton in the general election against Donald Trump. This is a claim that can be empirically tested, and it is objectively false. Bernie went all in for Clinton following her official nomination, holding an astonishing _39 rallies_ for her in the final months of the election. This was widely recognized at the time -- here's a sampling of major outlets' headlines in the fall of 2016.
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/3a969cb9-89a3-4953-a7c6-9903262f9a5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e22a17d7-45bc-4603-8fa3-7c8cea0b1378?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/63cc1ca8-17f1-4364-a6b8-5e176e12d541?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
It's also not true that Bernie's supporters disproportionately refused to vote for Clinton. Indeed, a [higher percentage](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/3a3a988c-6541-4203-b73a-f7cf07e81bce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Bernie's voters backed Clinton in 2016 than Clinton's supporters backed Barack Obama in the 2008 general election. The same applies to the 2020 election, when progressive candidates and voters rallied overwhelmingly behind Biden (though it's worth noting that Biden's winning campaign made much more of an effort to reach out to and engage these constituencies than Clinton's did). In each of these elections, progressives overwhelmingly accepted their disappointing primary losses and got to work supporting their non-preferred Democratic nominees in the general election.
A more recent example that establishment Democrats frequently invoke to accuse the party's left wing of insufficient loyalty is the Uncommitted campaign, an effort by Palestinian, Muslim, and progressive Democrats to push Biden to change his morally and politically disastrous support for Israel's war on Gaza by voting "uncommitted" in the 2024 Democratic primary. What these accusations ignore is that the Uncommitted campaign was explicitly a _primary_ strategy. As one of its organizers, Waleed Shahid, [explained](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9ebfc9ae-5c1c-4327-9c85-1917eb771b1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the effort was based on a "disciplined hypothesis: that a protest vote in the primaries, executed inside the party of which we were members, could force a recalibration early enough to matter. We weren't trying to blow up the coalition. We were trying to keep it alive." Compare this approach to that of the many establishment Democrats who worked hard to clear the 2024 primary of any credible alternatives to Biden -- an act of supposed party "loyalty" that proved unspeakably disastrous for the Democratic Party.
Of course, the Uncommitted campaign's hypothesis proved incorrect -- neither Biden nor Kamala Harris were ultimately swayed to make even the smallest possible effort to appeal to voters horrified by their unflagging support for Benjamin Netanyahu. And, as Uncommitted predicted and tried to prevent, this led to a [collapse](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/4d23206f-1b9b-4289-a20c-33bf4f2f5487?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Harris's support among Muslim voters, perhaps best exemplified by the eye-popping 117-point shift in the heavily Arab American city of Dearborn, Michigan, which [flipped](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/0cab44e1-b202-4d77-9880-09999a4d0dbd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from 88 percent support for Biden in 2020 to just 13 percent support for Harris in 2024.
And yet, despite Harris's utter rejection of their campaign, including even its most minor and [symbolic](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/65edc8cc-3cf2-4342-875f-8a0940b183e9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of demands, Uncommitted remained committed to defeating Trump, publicly urging supporters to vote for Harris in the general election on behalf, as Uncommitted leader and Palestinian state representative Ruwa Romman wrote in a _[Rolling Stone](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/769de3b5-3c6f-4d8b-8c59-19a2e90c9456?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_[ essay](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/769de3b5-3c6f-4d8b-8c59-19a2e90c9456?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) addressed to her community, of "the people in my district and state who cannot survive another Trump presidency."
| [](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1734842f-ea36-4c3b-b071-c635342aa2f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This gets to the fundamental contrast at issue here. The establishment deploys "Vote Blue No Matter Who" against progressives who try to use primary processes to move the party in a better direction, despite the fact that after those primaries -- which the left sadly loses far more often than it wins -- progressives invariably assume the posture of good Democratic soldiers. Centrists, on the other hand, scold progressives who are critical of the party establishment during primaries, but on the rare occasions they lose those primaries, they can't be counted on to reciprocate that support.
It's easy to say "Vote Blue No Matter Who" when that means voting for your own worldview, interests, and preferred Democratic candidates. It's a lot harder when it requires supporting someone you deeply oppose on issues that are critically important to you. Progressives have spent our entire lives swallowing our feelings to do the latter, and _still_ getting attacked by the establishment with accusations of disloyalty. But the moment the dynamic flips -- as occurred with Mamdani's victory in New York, where, it should be noted, he faces a contested general election against at least one candidate who is essentially a [puppet](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/eab14433-aca8-4afa-931f-76ee54067673?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Donald Trump, and another who's been practically [endorsed](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/1adc2595-3e4c-45bb-8e0d-979eb1fae578?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by him -- the establishment proves its loyalty is not to the Democratic Party, but to its own ironclad control of the party.
Rather than allow ourselves to be framed as an untrustworthy partner within the Democratic coalition, it's time for progressives to reclaim our role as the most committed core of that coalition: We represent, in the [words](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e0301c70-7be0-4230-ae03-8079768ed771?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the late Senator Paul Wellstone, the "Democratic wing of the Democratic Party."
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9hYXJvbnJlZ3VuYmVyZy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWNjb3VudCIsInAiOjE2ODUwMTc3MCwicyI6NDk4MTE3MCwiZiI6dHJ1ZSwidSI6OTA1MDExOTIsImlhdCI6MTc1Mjc1ODQzNCwiZXhwIjoyMDY4MzM0NDM0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMCIsInN1YiI6ImxpbmstcmVkaXJlY3QifQ.2CjksQfV_nAiPPKpTdQijw138tgYGIH4SyJzw4U_5B8?)

clean_body: A rule for thee, but not for me | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more A rule for thee, but not for me | | | Jul 17 | | | | | | | | | | | | I wanted to share this piece I wrote yesterday for . Warning: It's the kind of argument that might annoy some readers who identify more with the Democratic Party establishment. If that's you, and you're willing to read the piece even with that warning, you have my sincere appreciation. Please know, whether you agree with the conclusions or not, they reflect a frustration that's being felt right now by a whole lot of Democratic voters. So at the very least, I think it's worth keeping an open mind about. Thanks a million for taking a look. * Andrew Cuomo's back -- or rather, he's refusing to go away. This week. the former governor announced he will continue contesting the race for New York City mayor, challenging the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, who won the Democratic primary last month in a landslide, than any candidate has ever earned in the history of New York City Democratic primaries. Cuomo announced his general-election campaign in perhaps the cringiest way imaginable: with a that tried to ape Zohran's high-energy social media strategy and streetcorner appeal, but instead resembled a elderly hostage's proof-of-life. No wonder it got ; as of this writing, Cuomo's post had around 5,500 likes on Twitter, while Mamdani's below the video -- he simply shared a link for donations, sans commentary -- sits at over 180,000. | | ---|---|--- Cuomo's decision to run a third-party campaign against the Democratic nominee, after Democratic voters decisively rejected him in favor of that nominee, does not exist in a vacuum. It comes as a number of centrist Democratic leaders -- including Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, and New York Governor Kathy Hochul -- have refused to endorse Mamdani. Other Democrats have gone even further. New York Senator Kirsten Gillibrand went on an abrasively bigoted rant Zohran supported "global jihad." Centrist Dean Phillips said he there was no room in the Democratic Party for democratic socialists like Mamdani. And the list . These defections will likely have little effect on the actual general election results come November -- Zohran has significantly higher among voters than any of his critics. But the failure of so many centrist Democrats to rally around the Democratic nominee in a race to lead the biggest and most culturally dominant city in America does effectively illustrate an underrecognized and often misrepresented dynamic in Democratic politics: that it is actually the party's centrist establishment, not its progressive wing, that's most likely to violate the maxim of "Vote Blue No Matter Who." This is worth noting, because for years it's been centrists who have used "Vote Blue No Matter Who" as a cudgel to discipline progressives by equating criticism of the party establishment with a refusal to back Democrats in general elections. Trying to push the Party to change course on an issue -- to embrace universal healthcare, to support a ban on stock trading, to elevate younger leaders rather than double down on gerontocracy? Then you've probably at some point been scolded to "Vote Blue No Matter Who," as if pushing Democrats to be better is partisan treachery. But this charge has never been based in reality. In fact, the go-to examples used by centrists to scold progressives all militate in the opposite direction! Take Bernie Sanders. Now, for the love of god, nobody wants to relitigate 2016. But there are still many in the Democratic establishment -- from politicians to consultants to influencers -- who charge both Bernie and his supporters with not backing Clinton in the general election against Donald Trump. This is a claim that can be empirically tested, and it is objectively false. Bernie went all in for Clinton following her official nomination, holding an astonishing 39 rallies for her in the final months of the election. This was widely recognized at the time -- here's a sampling of major outlets' headlines in the fall of 2016. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- It's also not true that Bernie's supporters disproportionately refused to vote for Clinton. Indeed, a of Bernie's voters backed Clinton in 2016 than Clinton's supporters backed Barack Obama in the 2008 general election. The same applies to the 2020 election, when progressive candidates and voters rallied overwhelmingly behind Biden (though it's worth noting that Biden's winning campaign made much more of an effort to reach out to and engage these constituencies than Clinton's did). In each of these elections, progressives overwhelmingly accepted their disappointing primary losses and got to work supporting their non-preferred Democratic nominees in the general election. A more recent example that establishment Democrats frequently invoke to accuse the party's left wing of insufficient loyalty is the Uncommitted campaign, an effort by Palestinian, Muslim, and progressive Democrats to push Biden to change his morally and politically disastrous support for Israel's war on Gaza by voting "uncommitted" in the 2024 Democratic primary. What these accusations ignore is that the Uncommitted campaign was explicitly a primary strategy. As one of its organizers, Waleed Shahid, , the effort was based on a "disciplined hypothesis: that a protest vote in the primaries, executed inside the party of which we were members, could force a recalibration early enough to matter. We weren't trying to blow up the coalition. We were trying to keep it alive." Compare this approach to that of the many establishment Democrats who worked hard to clear the 2024 primary of any credible alternatives to Biden -- an act of supposed party "loyalty" that proved unspeakably disastrous for the Democratic Party. Of course, the Uncommitted campaign's hypothesis proved incorrect -- neither Biden nor Kamala Harris were ultimately swayed to make even the smallest possible effort to appeal to voters horrified by their unflagging support for Benjamin Netanyahu. And, as Uncommitted predicted and tried to prevent, this led to a in Harris's support among Muslim voters, perhaps best exemplified by the eye-popping 117-point shift in the heavily Arab American city of Dearborn, Michigan, which from 88 percent support for Biden in 2020 to just 13 percent support for Harris in 2024. And yet, despite Harris's utter rejection of their campaign, including even its most minor and of demands, Uncommitted remained committed to defeating Trump, publicly urging supporters to vote for Harris in the general election on behalf, as Uncommitted leader and Palestinian state representative Ruwa Romman wrote in a addressed to her community, of "the people in my district and state who cannot survive another Trump presidency." | | ---|---|--- This gets to the fundamental contrast at issue here. The establishment deploys "Vote Blue No Matter Who" against progressives who try to use primary processes to move the party in a better direction, despite the fact that after those primaries -- which the left sadly loses far more often than it wins -- progressives invariably assume the posture of good Democratic soldiers. Centrists, on the other hand, scold progressives who are critical of the party establishment during primaries, but on the rare occasions they lose those primaries, they can't be counted on to reciprocate that support. It's easy to say "Vote Blue No Matter Who" when that means voting for your own worldview, interests, and preferred Democratic candidates. It's a lot harder when it requires supporting someone you deeply oppose on issues that are critically important to you. Progressives have spent our entire lives swallowing our feelings to do the latter, and still getting attacked by the establishment with accusations of disloyalty. But the moment the dynamic flips -- as occurred with Mamdani's victory in New York, where, it should be noted, he faces a contested general election against at least one candidate who is essentially a of Donald Trump, and another who's been practically by him -- the establishment proves its loyalty is not to the Democratic Party, but to its own ironclad control of the party. Rather than allow ourselves to be framed as an untrustworthy partner within the Democratic coalition, it's time for progressives to reclaim our role as the most committed core of that coalition: We represent, in the of the late Senator Paul Wellstone, the "Democratic wing of the Democratic Party." ? | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Aaron Regunberg ? ?

subject: Why are they focused on THIS??
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Josh Weil, Democrat for US Senate
body:
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/387153444E713877456653376A556574376F314841345859327A4D665362374C42586753794B64635252546F6551413647436E7356536243345157355952664A/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-06-18%20at%2011.41.25%E2%80%AFAM.png]
While Florida families are watching their rents rise and groceries eat half their paycheck,
Fox News is out here debating whether they should “reclaim the word Nazi.”
Friend, That’s not a joke.
It’s just the latest outrage from a political machine that thrives on distraction.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/6E35552F5132482F66354E352F5657353332415970713471793056462B6D544C6C6D7864644A78343364566A6E723434776433626D6E32664E372F3633527262/editor_images/Screenshot%202025-07-17%20at%208.59.20%E2%80%AFAM.png]

❌Your health insurance just went up
❌ Your wages haven’t kept up
❌ And your tax dollars are being handed to the wealthy
This is the game, and we’re not playing it. We know the only way to get rid of this rhetoric, is to do it ourselves and replace those in power.
I’m running for U.S. Senate to do something radical:
✅ Focus on your actual life.
✅ Fight for the public schools, housing, and wages our communities deserve.
✅ Build an economy that works for people, not just corporations.

body:
We’re celebrating America’s 250th

Patriot, I’m excited to announce that in celebration of America’s 250th birthday, I’m giving away one of my favorite guns!
**The FN M249S PARA is one of the most iconic guns in American history, and now you have a chance to win mine!**
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
[ENTER NOW](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
For two and a half centuries, we’ve fought off tyrants, defended our Constitution, and kept the flame of freedom burning bright. Now, it’s time to honor that legacy with a giveaway that screams AMERICAN PRIDE.
**The M249 SAW PARA is the perfect embodiment of American greatness... and it could be YOURS.** **_[Are you ready to claim it](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)_?**
[ENTER NOW](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
I’m so excited to celebrate 250 years of Independence for our beloved country, and I would love for you to **_[celebrate with me by entering for your chance to win](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)_!**
_In Liberty,_
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
**Marjorie Taylor Greene**
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)

name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: I want a chance to earn your trust
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I bet this wasn’t on your radar when you woke up this morning |

---
Peter,

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to.

Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: _**Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss.**_ While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos.

Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away.

I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— _**just weeks after 9/11.**_

_**I didn’t wait. I went.**_

And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map.

I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter.

Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign.
_**

[**HELP ME PROTECT THE AMERICA WE LOVE
CHIP IN $10 TODAY**](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=cc0d7b88e42b4ca6ad8b695d02cca7eb&_e=A68-k3J2RYyov7IgMY_tqnt2CAPcp6YoVIjK3pde2-Il3FuqENFMD0cRq_Q-dxNl6zgfoPIJk8USxtHRRN0ljR5dxZ2wQMVRR9btHszC0non_aXPprv0E0lOBm9cdhEK4MonqzdIA4rg7Diepl9ismxRTxUbYC-4H8LNWiZaBQz3_iFqcF2oaFXFmtqG14W1sDi-hUJIBrHFpuLC2-X-5DoUJAjc61FMex167Z7PCP1N1K1YkUIw8wA4LQmcafimn14CzjA1BgZ-8H2vtQBfePdel53hCDd3KRWiJizjf2Bnv8uWz903AQMnXq0mib5rMYVpLLFdMTcnUXggLJCU3jGsK3dvRsKyHHrrX9zMQUwbcWJmThAmMT7qXQ3kIDRHfXsvAxbQY3r5fVZ3pOHQA2bPtuviIfdZWpS3yqR7Sf_peniDw2y-RM2jrqlBHGIj_RBF_tmZxXXOSXxrzmPksiprQzXYgQ1Y-6E3fGaqvxgrjgw8GIOeGbN88pRW086OahODg0IENIhf1IbcOnXwEhscef-b2uE3bR6YmSCHUdCgl6FRiRcjvozs_mdIhhCPNqbVDRHkzCa7S-6rg_SoE5xhy5QKuVivXwJrIng_GMesXVAULN5rtWMYolueZoDq2nhvHAEa7bY4vQtkWGhVlH-loHzyDO1nXCV5EkNmDnOYgLH0G5oufun0Wv6rYyQ1d5bgKHrzKjB-AQyQmjwFREik1fg9hQX1aNTUAlf1-uPZxqKgmZiX7v26rj9aqzEbBrO0yoffWDfQaqMloAUComozjz57EUGKQ1B103HOcZU%3D)
**_ **_Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win._**

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. **But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for.**

And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for.

We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest.

**If you believe in what I’m fighting for,[even $5 goes a long way.](http://links.standingstrongamerica.com/u/click?_t=29c3b6e742c849fabbcc130432510e66&_m=cc0d7b88e42b4ca6ad8b695d02cca7eb&_e=A68-k3J2RYyov7IgMY_tqnt2CAPcp6YoVIjK3pde2-Il3FuqENFMD0cRq_Q-dxNl6zgfoPIJk8USxtHRRN0ljR5dxZ2wQMVRR9btHszC0non_aXPprv0E0lOBm9cdhEK4MonqzdIA4rg7Diepl9ismxRTxUbYC-4H8LNWiZaBQz3_iFqcF2oaFXFmtqG14W1sDi-hUJIBrHFpuLC2-X-5DoUJAjc61FMex167Z7PCP1N1K1YkUIw8wA4LQmcafimn14CzjA1BgZ-8H2vtQBfePdel53hCDd3KRWiJizjf2DYjvYNG4m9_ZDVt_Z0nbegAFqhmRo_coJIaRSCHqCyxNIcLxPboErrWpoUUy1CjVY2tACXZ5pg3IWSXYlfLjUoF4MteKUjcxR07LuFcWt_kUywluY7WSER63RV2e1wR-3a0QAuzAACwmts2i4vcyKpx3edAk9F9Bb_ZiNa3Df4rrKd5GEG4SS-nYjqT_Q5LhD-kB_79wksJ1Y2EHpDzyynLWGU7ZGHoWBqdxKMiJi_KJzkPvn3CR0JAoI3O2E3GbsfKitkGFGc1XoyTaLrOT0UIgZosRtLXvQ0HZNfI8aAZ1_hh3wOsbqiuj0oqGoir4jgC_TIZVhgu_hz51XON14v_B6MSH1cOfLvzDNJtTYOgX8ieespuOCSfeZo7ahBGO7cJhF5_oYRgok-_Lv0MfgTTgtlh4XstLhRfbTHmxQar39UJsVgXTUkgT9RU39dUkhI9OVY2MTFezjpO-opunhwIeNdMr70G7sy9OWzP_wer4HuDfDoLBnX0u638WkskvQ%3D)**

If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now.

Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together.

clean_body: I bet this wasn’t on your radar when you woke up this morning | Peter, I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to. Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss. While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos. Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— just weeks after 9/11. I didn’t wait. I went. And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map. I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter. Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign. Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for. We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest. If you believe in what I’m fighting for, If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now. Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together. You’ve done your part for this country. In liberty Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

body:
Donald Trump and the GOP’s big, “beautiful” bill is not even remotely beautiful.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111637658/557894944/155360260?refcode=header&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODQ0Yjg3OC0xNjYzLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hMe6Op4ga5d2yI7TbZsk4a8VSeEEwocyB_9FLG31IiA=)
---
Peter,
I’ve said it already and I’ll say it again, folks: **Donald Trump and the GOP’s big, “beautiful” bill is not even remotely beautiful—it’s one of the biggest steps backwards our country has ever seen.**
Their big, ugly bill forces an extreme, unpopular agenda onto the American people, and millions of working families will pay a steep price. It will steal away their health care, food assistance, and other critical government services, and dramatically raise their cost of living—all just to make some of the richest people on earth even richer.
**Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans are not serving working Americans. They are only serving themselves and their billionaire buddies. And I’ll keep shouting it from the rooftops for as long as I have to.**
I’m doing everything I can to fight back because Minnesotans deserve so much better. This kind of greed and cruelty is not what Minnesotans believe in, and I’ll defend the values we do stand for—like compassion, fairness, and opportunity—at every turn.
**But I need your help in this fight, Peter. It takes a strong, united movement of everyday people like you to fight back and defeat an agenda as dangerous as Trump’s.**
[**Will you chip in today to our movement to help me keep up the fight against the Trump administration and stand up for Minnesota values?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111637660/557894945/216031234?refcode=body&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDc0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODQ0Yjg3OC0xNjYzLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=hMe6Op4ga5d2yI7TbZsk4a8VSeEEwocyB_9FLG31IiA=)

clean_body: Donald Trump and the GOP’s big, “beautiful” bill is not even remotely beautiful. | = Peter, I’ve said it already and I’ll say it again, folks: Donald Trump and the GOP’s big, “beautiful” bill is not even remotely beautiful—it’s one of the biggest steps backwards our country has ever seen. Their big, ugly bill forces an extreme, unpopular agenda onto the American people, and millions of working families will pay a steep price. It will steal away their health care, food assistance, and other critical government services, and dramatically raise their cost of living—all just to make some of the richest people on earth even richer. Donald Trump and MAGA Republicans are not serving working Americans. They are only serving themselves and their billionaire buddies. And I’ll keep shouting it from the rooftops for as long as I have to. I’m doing everything I can to fight back because Minnesotans deserve so much better. This kind of greed and cruelty is not what Minnesotans believe in, and I’ll defend the values we do stand for—like compassion, fairness, and opportunity—at every turn. But I need your help in this fight, Peter. It takes a strong, united movement of everyday people like you to fight back and defeat an agenda as dangerous as Trump’s. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = I'm grateful, Tim Walz ********=******

name: Ryan Friedrichs
subject: Jocelyn’s biggest fan
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
To know Jocelyn is to know a warrior.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
Hi there, this is Ryan — Jocelyn’s husband and #1 supporter.
To know Jocelyn is to know a warrior.
As a military spouse during deployment, a working mom, a defender of democracy, and a Tigers fan, she never gives up, backs down, or blinks. And she won’t for Michigan families as Governor.
[**Now, before asking for your support — which I will — let me tell you a little about me.**](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q32025.OcO0Lx&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
| [DONATE](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
---
I was born and raised in Michigan and attended the University of Michigan — though I was raised by two Spartan parents, one born all the way up in Marquette.
I met Jocelyn in graduate school, and we moved back to Detroit when she began her clerkship for Judge Damon Keith, who married us on Belle Isle, after we’d gotten engaged all the way up in Isle Royale National Park.
[](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)_Jocelyn, and our son Aiden, share my love for the Detroit Tigers._
When I served our country as a combat veteran, Army paratrooper, Sergeant, and infantry Team Leader with the 173rd Airborne Brigade, she founded the Military Spouses of Michigan to provide support and services to families like ours.
[](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
I was Jocelyn’s biggest cheerleader when she first ran for Secretary of State, just as she was mine all the years I was overseas. Her unshakable belief in real opportunity and justice for everyone, no matter their zip code, or income level — and no matter the attacks she endures — has always inspired me.
[](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=6&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
Now, as she runs for Governor, focused on making Michigan the best place to be a kid, raise a kid, and call home, our son, Aiden, and I (and our dog Loba) have her back.
[](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=7&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
[**Now I have to ask: will you have her back too? Her next fundraising deadline is coming up. Will you pitch for her fight for Governor? Your support means so much to our whole family.**](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/269?c0=jmb-em-q32025.plcv4o&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=8&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/263?c0=jmb-em-q32025._u867d&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=9&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
---

---

---
[CHIP IN $100 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/266?c0=jmb-em-q32025.z9_9cy&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=12&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)
---

---
[ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250717_PM1_V1_S1&t=14&akid=491%2E181023%2EMXG96d)

Thank you for supporting Jocelyn — we’re lucky to have you on the team.
— Ryan

| | | [](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9Yr19exo8ATdRfiqRDml-2FPz7cnf2nT45L4TDLL79xclFmlYUKVz-2FZIWg-2FtTcyX6kz-2FubRbqYbAFr6fPtsXt6CN8Y7KVo8DjxRiV5Wrf27xQ3j3XWiIHbrezdQ-2BDnEHnAIpBqWDzlnU8qjrQeDdHu3icU-3DiFgB_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVAKmTIthZAqug-2BBe3TY9jweA5gYJTWBMeK2mbEu1GsUAWbMxqbbanoccXqIiueqpc6DIzHn4ElffI7A3Y14vKdCNksTNdqpcPsZ-2Fywu-2FMuXA4y3CGiU9X4tiAddJTMlizHBV3fCrFnIJPehcpV1HfrYKHS5RBvjN-2F4gU98MJ1oW-2FmRP2UZacXIqOfb5iQtX8Si8FNwLSgT9q-2F-2FUWlkG0fDf5adgldwO86acrs4N0xNF90twMmdV3LBcPzxs3uYwMmTZMvQIMTk-2FChdyQU2Ub8zt5-2B6JBj9V9qLXXyyQnyarv08k-2B04DB3M7drqyuDJiHXS744LOi8vch29S-2F1qHTSOT)
